story.iWant: to create new local calendar events by dragging in week/day view or by using a toolbar button
story.soThat: I can capture unpublished events immediately and finish editing them in the same details panel flow
story.tasks: - Add the planned Tier 2 pending_event EF Core path instead of trying to store new drafts in gcal_event - Widen the current Google-only shared event identity contracts so pending drafts can flow through query, selection, and details-panel logic - Extend the shared calendar query service to return both synced Google events and pending local drafts in the active range - Add drag-to-create gesture handling and live preview overlays to DayViewControl and WeekViewControl using 15-minute snapping - Add a + Add Event toolbar button in MainPage that gathers date/time defaults, creates the pending draft, refreshes the range, selects it, and opens the details panel in edit mode

acceptanceCriteria.ac: Given the user is in Week or Day view and drags on empty timeline space, a new local-only event draft is created for the snapped drag range when the pointer is released.
Given the user drags to create, both the start and end of the created event snap to 15-minute boundaries, and a drag shorter than one slot still produces at least one 15-minute block.
Given a new event is created by drag or button, the event becomes the active selection and the right-side details panel opens immediately in edit mode for that newly created draft.

Given the user clicks + Add Event, a lightweight date/time prompt opens with start time prefilled to the current local time rounded to the nearest 15 minutes and end time defaulted to one hour later; confirming creates the new draft and opens it in the details/edit flow.
Given a new event is created, it is persisted as a pending_event row with a generated pending ID, no Google event ID yet, app_created = true, source_system = manual, and ready_to_publish = false.
Given a new draft exists in the visible range, it appears immediately using the default Azure colour and 60% opacity with a visible local-only status such as Not yet published to Google Calendar.
Given the user starts a drag but presses Esc, releases outside the active day column, or otherwise cancels before completion, no record is created and no phantom preview remains on screen.

Given the user clicks an existing Google-synced event, current selection, tooltip, navigation, and details-panel behavior continue to work; creation logic does not steal input from existing event blocks.

Given the visible date range contains both synced gcal_event rows and local pending_event rows, the shared query/display pipeline returns both, ordered correctly by start time, without duplicating or dropping either source.
Unit and integration tests cover pending-event persistence, 15-minute snapping math, and range queries that mix synced and pending events.

doc.title: Story 3.6 (primary reference)
doc.snippet: Primary implementation guide. Resolves the critical mismatch between Google-only event contracts in the live repo and the Tier 2 requirement to create unpublished local drafts.

doc.title: Epic breakdown
doc.snippet: Authoritative user-story language for drag-to-create and + Add Event, including Azure defaults, 15-minute snapping, and local-only unpublished behavior.

doc.title: Epic 3 technical specification
doc.snippet: Important guardrail: Epic 3 originally states future model extensions must not assume GcalEventId is the only event source. Story 3.6 is the first place that becomes operationally true.

doc.title: UX design specification
doc.section: Event Creation, Visual State Language, Tier 2 journey
doc.snippet: Defines the UX for drag-to-create, + Add Event defaults, and 60% opacity for unpublished drafts.

doc.title: Tier 2 requirements
doc.section: Event Creation, Visual State Indicators, Workflow
doc.snippet: Confirms the Tier 2 workflow: drag or button creation, instant local persistence, and translucent unpublished state.

doc.title: Database schemas
doc.snippet: Authoritative schema source for pending_event. Story 3.6 must use this table for new drafts because gcal_event is the synced Google cache and requires a non-null Google event ID.

doc.title: Architecture document
doc.section: ADR-014 Separate pending_event Table, Tier 2 editing and publishing
doc.snippet: Reinforces the separate-table design for unpublished local drafts and the 60% opacity UI model. Treat this as the architectural source of truth when it conflicts with the narrower 3.5 draft.

doc.title: Documentation alignment summary
doc.section: Feature reassignments, event creation methods, visual feedback system
doc.snippet: Confirms Story 3.6 sits in Tier 2 and that drag creation and the + Add Event button are both intended, with pending drafts rendered translucently.

doc.title: Story 3.4
doc.snippet: Defines the shared details-panel host location in MainPage and the selection-driven open/close path that Story 3.6 must reuse for new drafts.

doc.title: Story 3.5
doc.snippet: Important dependency context. Its current draft is Google-event-centric and must be widened so the same panel/edit flow can save pending drafts as well.

file.reason: Currently exposes only Tier 1 tables. Story 3.6 must add DbSet<PendingEvent> and a matching configuration so EF can create and query pending_event.
Confirms gcal_event is keyed by non-null string GcalEventId and already represents the synced Google cache, not an unpublished local-draft store.
Shows gcal_event_id is the primary key with ValueGeneratedNever(). This is the concrete reason Story 3.6 must not force new drafts into gcal_event.
Currently uses GcalEventId as if every visible event were Google-backed. Story 3.6 must widen this shared display model to represent pending drafts too.
Currently exposes GetEventByGcalIdAsync(...). Story 3.6 must generalize this query path to source-agnostic event identity instead of adding a second pending-only details lookup.
Today it maps only GcalEvent rows. Story 3.6 must union gcal_event and pending_event while preserving local-time projection and ordering.
Its SelectedGcalEventId naming is too narrow for Tier 2 creation. Widen this shared contract before pending drafts start flowing through selection and panel logic.
Reused source of truth for event selection. Story 3.6 should still route selection through this service; it just needs source-agnostic event IDs instead of Google-only naming.
Currently carries GcalEventId. Widen the payload meaning before using it to open the panel on newly created pending drafts.
Main shared range owner. Story 3.6 must reuse its refresh path after creation, and any event-change message introduced here should be generic enough for both update and create flows.
Owns the toolbar where + Add Event belongs and hosts the shared shell that also contains the details panel overlay path from Story 3.4.
Add the button flow here and keep shell-level keyboard handling consistent with the existing accelerator pattern.
Current timed-event rendering is hour-granular and imperative. Story 3.6 must add empty-space drag hit targets and 15-minute preview/render math here.
Same drag-to-create and quarter-hour rendering problem as WeekViewControl, but simpler because it only hosts one day column.
Even though month view does not support drag creation, it must still render pending drafts distinctly once the shared query path includes them.
Reference test style for view-model work. Extend or mirror this pattern for any creation-triggered range refresh behavior.
Reference integration pattern for local database-backed query tests. Story 3.6 should add pending-event union coverage alongside the existing gcal_event tests.

package.name: Microsoft.WindowsAppSDK
package.use: WinUI 3 controls, Pointer events, ContentDialog, DatePicker, TimePicker, and visual-layer primitives needed for drag previews.

package.name: CommunityToolkit.Mvvm
package.use: Existing observable/view-model and messenger infrastructure reused by selection and details-panel coordination.

package.name: Microsoft.EntityFrameworkCore.Sqlite
package.use: Tier 2 migration plus repository/query work for pending_event.

dotnet.newPackagesNeeded: None. Story 3.6 should use the packages already referenced by the app and test projects.

constraints.constraint: Project structure is flat at the repo root. New files belong under Data/, Services/, Views/, ViewModels/, Models/, or Messages/ directly. Do not create Core/, src/, or a second assembly.
Do not store newly created unpublished drafts in gcal_event. The non-null string primary key and synced-cache semantics make that contract wrong for Story 3.6.
Add the planned pending_event table through EF Core before or alongside the first creation flow. Story 3.6 is the first Tier 2 story that operationally requires it.
Do not add a parallel pending-only selection or query path. Widen the existing shared event identity contracts so synced and pending events use the same shell/panel flow.
Drag-to-create is Week and Day view only. Do not add drag creation to Month or Year view.
Current hour-only timed-event placement is insufficient. Do not declare Story 3.6 done unless newly created drafts render at quarter-hour precision, not just hour buckets.
The + Add Event flow must round the default start time to the nearest 15-minute boundary and default the end time to one hour later.
New pending drafts must be local-only: app_created = true, source_system = manual, ready_to_publish = false, no Google event ID yet.
Pending drafts must render at 60% opacity with Azure default colour and an unpublished status label. Do not hide the local-only state inside one view or panel only.
Reuse the right-side details/edit panel path from Stories 3.4 and 3.5. Create the pending record first, then select it, then open it in edit mode.
Selection ownership remains in CalendarSelectionService and EventSelectedMessage. Creation logic must not introduce a second active-event state machine.
Do not query CalendarDbContext directly from the view code-behind. Views should translate completed drag gestures into service/repository operations only.
Do not build separate persistence models for drag creation and button creation. Both input methods must converge on the same pending-event creation path and shared rendering/panel flow.

tests.standards: xUnit plus FluentAssertions. Use real SQLite-backed integration tests for pending_event persistence and mixed-source query behavior. Keep drag-slot math in a small helper or service so it can be unit-tested without UI automation.

ideas.test: Insert a new draft through the pending-event repository and assert PendingEventId uses the pending_ prefix, CalendarId is primary, AppCreated is true, SourceSystem is manual, and ReadyToPublish is false.
Seed one gcal_event row and one pending_event row in the same day range, load through the shared query service, and assert both appear with the correct source metadata and ordering.
Seed a pending_event row and assert the mapped CalendarEventDisplayModel carries IsPending = true, Opacity = 0.6, and the expected unpublished status label.
Feed drag start/end values through a shared snapping helper and assert both ends snap to 15-minute boundaries with a minimum duration of one slot.
Fix the current time in a test seam, create button-flow defaults, and assert the start time rounds to a 15-minute boundary and end = start + one hour.
Simulate the cancel path in the creation service or coordinator and assert no pending_event row is inserted and no refresh/select message is emitted.
